name: build
context: Build production bundles with Vite — targets, minification (oxc/terser), sourcemaps, library mode, SSR, code splitting, manifest generation
info: This skill covers Vite's production build pipeline powered by Rolldown. Key build options: build.target defaults to 'baseline-widely-available' (broadly supported modern features) but accepts ES versions (es2020), browser versions (chrome100, firefox115), and Node versions (node20); build.outDir defaults to 'dist'; build.assetsDir defaults to 'assets'; build.assetsInlineLimit (4096 bytes) controls when small assets are base64-inlined; build.cssCodeSplit (true) emits CSS per async chunk instead of one monolithic file; build.cssMinify defaults to 'lightningcss' for fast CSS minification; build.minify defaults to 'oxc' which is 30-90x faster than terser while producing comparable output — set to 'terser' for maximum compression or false to disable; build.sourcemap accepts false/true/'inline'/'hidden' for debugging production issues; build.rollupOptions (or build.rolldownOptions) exposes the underlying Rolldown bundler config for input/output/plugin customization; build.lib configures library mode with entry, name, formats (es/cjs/umd/iife), and fileName; build.manifest generates a manifest.json mapping source files to hashed output files for backend integration; build.ssrManifest generates a manifest for SSR preload directives; build.ssr sets the SSR entry point producing a Node-compatible bundle; build.emptyOutDir clears outDir before each build (defaults true when outDir is inside project root); build.copyPublicDir (true) copies the public/ directory; build.reportCompressedSize (true) reports gzip sizes but slows builds on large projects; build.chunkSizeWarningLimit (500 kB) warns on oversized chunks; build.watch enables Rolldown watch mode for incremental rebuilds; and build.modulePreload controls the module preload polyfill. Use [[vite/config]] to set these options in vite.config.ts, [[vite/plugins]] for build-time plugins like legacy browser support, and [[vite/env]] for environment-specific build modes.

sections.title: Basic production build
sections.body: Run `vite build` to bundle the project into `dist/`. Vite reads `index.html` as the entry point, resolves all `<script type="module">` imports, tree-shakes unused code, code-splits on dynamic imports, hashes filenames for caching, and minifies JS/CSS. Use `--mode <name>` to load environment-specific `.env.<mode>` files (see [[vite/env]]). Use `--outDir <path>` to change the output directory. The `public/` folder is copied as-is to the output root.

sections.title: Build targets and browser support
sections.body: The `build.target` option determines the JavaScript syntax level of output. Defaults to 'baseline-widely-available' which targets broadly supported modern features. Accepts ES version strings (es2015-es2024), specific browser versions (chrome100, firefox115, safari16, edge88), or 'esnext' for latest syntax. For Node targets use 'node20' etc. Under the hood Vite uses Oxc for transpilation. For legacy browser support (IE11, old Safari) install @vitejs/plugin-legacy from [[vite/plugins]].

sections.title: Library mode
sections.body: Set `build.lib` to produce a distributable library instead of an application. Configure `entry` (the library entry point), `name` (global variable for UMD/IIFE), `formats` (array of 'es', 'cjs', 'umd', 'iife'), and `fileName` (function or string for output names). Externalize framework dependencies like React or Vue via `build.rollupOptions.external` so consumers provide their own. Library mode does not generate type declarations — use [[typescript/compiler]] tsc with `--emitDeclarationOnly` for `.d.ts` output. Configure `package.json` exports with `main`, `module`, and `exports` fields.

sections.title: Code splitting and chunk strategies
sections.body: Vite automatically creates separate chunks for dynamic imports (`import('./module')` and `React.lazy()`). Customize chunking with `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` — pass an object mapping chunk names to module arrays, or a function that returns a chunk name for each module ID. Common strategy: group all `node_modules` into a `vendor` chunk for better caching. Use `build.chunkSizeWarningLimit` (default 500 kB) to flag oversized chunks. CSS is code-split per async chunk when `build.cssCodeSplit` is true (default).

sections.title: Source maps configuration
sections.body: Control source map generation with `build.sourcemap`: `false` (default, no maps), `true` (separate `.map` files alongside output), `'inline'` (maps embedded in output files — large, debug only), `'hidden'` (generates `.map` files but does not add the `//# sourceMappingURL` comment — ideal for uploading to Sentry/Datadog without exposing maps to browsers). Source maps increase build time and output size; enable only when needed for production debugging or error tracking.

sections.title: SSR builds
sections.body: Set `build.ssr` to a server entry point to produce a Node-compatible SSR bundle. Typically you run two build passes: `vite build --outDir dist/client` for the client bundle and `vite build --outDir dist/server --ssr src/entry-server.ts` for the server bundle. Set `build.target` to 'node20' (or appropriate Node version) for SSR output. Enable `build.ssrManifest` to generate a manifest mapping modules to preload directives for the HTML shell. Enable `build.manifest` for the client build so the server can resolve hashed asset URLs. See [[vite/dev-server]] for SSR middleware mode during development.

related.note: Build options are configured inside vite.config.ts using defineConfig
Use tsc for type declarations in library mode — Vite only transpiles, does not emit .d.ts

scripts.name: Build Vite Project
scripts.description: Run vite build with configurable mode, sourcemaps, output directory, and size reporting

PROJECT_DIR.description: Path to the Vite project root

SOURCEMAP.description: Enable sourcemaps: true, false, inline, hidden (default: false)

project_dir.description: Path to the Vite project root directory

mode.description: Build mode — controls which .env file is loaded

sourcemap.description: Source map generation: false, true, 'inline', or 'hidden'

outDir.description: Output directory relative to project root

target.description: Build target: ES version, browser versions, or 'esnext'

outDir.description: Absolute path to the build output directory

totalSize.description: Total size of the build output (human-readable)
